steps.title: Identify angle A and the right angle in the triangle
steps.content: Angle A is at the bottom-left vertex. The right angle is at vertex C (marked with a small square). This means C is the vertex where the two legs meet at 90°.

steps.title: Identify the sides relative to angle A
steps.content:
From angle A's perspective:
  • Opposite side (across from A): BC = 20 (vertical leg)
  • Adjacent side (next to A): AC = 21 (horizontal leg)

steps.title: Apply the tangent ratio
steps.content:
The tangent of an angle in a right triangle is the ratio of opposite to adjacent:
$$\tan A = \frac{\text{opposite}}{\text{adjacent}} = \frac{20}{21}$$
That matches choice C — C is correct.
